verdict: FAIL-CLOSED — single remaining runtime compatibility blocker: the wrapper `mlx_lm.models.qwen3_5.TextModel` class (the one that `model.language_model` actually instantiates) does not implement `rollback_speculative_cache`. The ArraysCache shape-strict rollback works on the inner `Qwen3_5TextModel`, but the dflash boundary validator looks for the hook on the outer wrapper. Resource gate and preflight both pass; the failure is in `validate_dflash_runtime_compatibility`, not in preflight. Returning to orchestrator with exact logs per the feature description; retrying heavier loads without first patching the runtime compatibility surface is out of scope.
outstanding_followups: Patch mlx_lm.models.qwen3_5.TextModel (the wrapper class that model.language_model actually instantiates) so it exposes rollback_speculative_cache by delegating to self.model.rollback_speculative_cache, OR rebind TextModel to PatchedQwen3_5TextModel directly inside mlx_engine/model_kit/patches/qwen3_5.py:apply_patches().

After the wrapper TextModel exposes rollback_speculative_cache, the dflash_stream_generate call path at mlx_engine/utils/dflash_runtime.py line 422-429 should reach the rollback hook successfully and complete the first draft/verify round.

Re-run the exact same capped smoke command after the wrapper TextModel is patched; expect error=null, output_preview containing the assistant answer (e.g. 'ok'), completion_tokens between 1 and 16, and the same telemetry block.

The 16 KVCache + 48 ArraysCache shape-strict check is now operational and works for the real Qwen3.6 27B target. Only the TextModel wrapper needs the rollback method; no broad ragged-cache enablement is required.

Until the wrapper TextModel.rollback_speculative_cache is implemented, the capped smoke cannot complete on the available Qwen3.6 target. There is no alternative target path on this host (only Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit and the promotion-blocked MoE Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-MLX-8bit are present).
